blocks.content: Mr. Cole pulled the Town Car to the curb and put it in park. He didn't say anything. He never had to.

Larry Jenkins sat in the back seat for three seconds longer than he needed to. Through the window the school looked the way every school looked on the first day — like something that had already decided what it thought of you.
Larry opened the door. The Town Car pulled away without ceremony. He stood on the sidewalk with his backpack and watched the crowd move around him like water around a rock.
He was looking for one face.
